summary: QuEra Computing and Roadrunner Venture Studios have announced a $4 million partnership to build a quantum testbed at the Roadrunner Quantum Lab in Albuquerque, New Mexico, with support from the State of New Mexico's $300 million quantum economy initiative. QuEra will establish facilities and hire full-time staff, bringing its neutral-atom quantum computing platform to the region. The partnership aims to provide advanced infrastructure and workforce training, supporting startup formation and technological validation for national laboratories, industry, and academia. Facilities will include a photonics and optics testing center and a classical computing user-access lab, initially serving academic and lab partners before broader access. This move reinforces New Mexico’s status as a growing hub for quantum research and commercialization.
